member.name: Sir Julian Smith
member.fullTitle: Rt Hon Sir Julian Smith MP
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Sir Julian Smith is the Conservative MP for Skipton and Ripon , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Visitor Levy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made with Cabinet colleagues of the potential impact of the introduction of an overnight visitor levy on hospitality businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has announced powers for Mayors to introduce a visitor levy on short-term overnight accommodation in their region, to drive economic growth, including through support for the local visitor economy. The precise design and scope of the power ...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Between 2022 and 2026, England’s 44 Protected Landscapes, including the Yorkshire Dales, received £15.47 million capital funding under the Access for All programme. The programme was designed to make Protected Landscapes more accessible to people of all a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Yorkshire Dales National Park Authority: Standards

writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra monitors and reviews delivery of each National Park Authority, in line with its grant agreement and their statutory National Park Management Plan. These plans are developed and agreed in partnership with local organisations and communities and show ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Yorkshire Dales National Park Authority: Standards

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether she is considering reducing VAT for the hospitality sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: VAT is a broad-based tax on consumption, and the 20 per cent standard rate applies to most goods and services. Reduced rates of VAT come at a significant cost to the Exchequer and reduce the revenue available for vital public services. Any reduction in ta...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: North Yorkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of how the hospitality sector supports local economies and tourism in North Yorkshire, and what support is available to help the sector remain resilient in the long term.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has not made a specific assessment of how the hospitality sector supports local economies and tourism in North Yorkshire. The Government recognises the hospitality sector’s vital role in supporting local economies, tourism, and high streets...

writtenQuestions.heading: Clinical Trials: North Yorkshire

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is committed to increasing clinical research activity across all parts of England, including North Yorkshire, to ensure patients have equitable access to opportunities to take part in research.The Department invests in research through the ...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department funds health and care research via its research operational arm, the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR), across England. The Department is committed to ensuring that research is inclusive and representative of the popula...

writtenQuestions.heading: Competition: Civil Proceedings

writtenQuestions.heading: Business Rates: Tax Allowances
